structuralPatterns.pattern: Five-paragraph narrative arc (Setup → Rising Action → Climax → Falling Action → Resolution)
structuralPatterns.description: Every model essay is scaffolded on a clear narrative trajectory, moving from an established setting through escalating tension to a climax, followed by a resolution and reflection.

examples.quote: Setup: at the mall. Rising: bought ice cream. Climax: lost wallet. Falling: info counter. Resolution: found it.
Setup: broken toy. Rising: anger subsides. Climax: birthday surprise. Falling: discovers brother's sacrifice. Resolution: warm embrace.

structuralPatterns.pattern: Opening hook — in-the-moment dialogue, action, or sensory detail (NOT 'One sunny day…')
structuralPatterns.description: Examiners are immediately pulled into the scene through active dialogue, a specific physical action, or an intriguing sensory detail rather than generic weather reports.

examples.quote: "What's this?" I asked, waving an envelope I had found on the dining table.
"Grrr..." Kim shot a knowing look at her sister Jolie. Jolie smiled back sheepishly and patted her rumbling stomach...
"Have you set up an appointment yet?" I prodded my mother again as she sipped her coffee, phone in one hand.

structuralPatterns.pattern: Climax = a SHIFT OF EMOTION / inner turning point (not just a dramatic event)
structuralPatterns.description: The true climax of the story relies on the protagonist's internal emotional shift—such as moving from panic to resolve, or anger to empathy—rather than just the external action.

examples.quote: He resisted the urge to panic and remembered his mother's instructions to go to the information counter whenever he needed help.
However, I did not have the heart to do so after seeing his pitiful expression. My anger subsided and I forgot about it...
His heart was touched by the stories of rescued dogs and cats finding forever homes, thanks to the run.

structuralPatterns.pattern: Conclusion echoes / restates the title or theme word(s)
structuralPatterns.description: The final sentences explicitly loop back to the year's theme word or phrase, proving to the examiner that the essay stayed entirely on topic.

examples.quote: ...I looked at my team members in appreciation, proud of the teamwork we had displayed in working together to win the competition.
Tommy had changed for the better, and it all began with that simple history project.
Trying something new was not bad at all!

structuralPatterns.pattern: Lesson / reflection appears in the final paragraph (the takeaway)
structuralPatterns.description: The protagonist reflects on the moral of the story or what they learned from the experience, providing a mature, thoughtful takeaway to close the narrative.

examples.quote: That simple train ride taught me that kindness does not need to be grand. Even giving up a seat can make a big difference.
It had special significance, for it was a symbol of my brother's love for me. That was something no amount of money in the world could buy.

craftRows.weak: I walked to the door. I felt nervous. I opened it slowly.
craftRows.strong: Heart pounding, I crept towards the door. Slowly — almost reluctantly — I turned the knob.

craftRows.category: Replace flat statements of emotion with body-language / sensation (show-don't-tell)
craftRows.weak: I was very angry when she said that.
craftRows.strong: Her words hit me like a slap. My fists clenched and my jaw tightened.
craftRows.highlight: My fists clenched and my jaw tightened

craftRows.weak: I looked at the big burger and ate it quickly.
craftRows.strong: I stared at the humongous burger and eagerly sank my teeth into it.

craftRows.category: Add sensory detail to flat description
craftRows.weak: The beach was nice and we had good food.
craftRows.strong: The cooling sea breeze blew in my face as the mouth-watering aroma of food wafted through the air.
craftRows.highlight: cooling sea breeze blew in my face

craftRows.weak: We went home. The next day was my birthday.
craftRows.strong: My anger subsided and I forgot about it after a few days. It so happened that my birthday was a few weeks after that.

craftRows.category: Slow down at the climax with short punchy sentences
craftRows.weak: I looked in my pocket and the shopping cart but my wallet was gone so I panicked.
craftRows.strong: I dug around my pocket. Nothing. I peered into the shopping cart. It was nowhere to be found!
craftRows.highlight: Nothing. I peered into the shopping cart.

craftRows.category: Replace a generic ending with a title-echoing reflective close
craftRows.weak: We went home happily. It was a fun day.
craftRows.strong: As the car drove off, I smiled in contentment. Trying something new was not bad at all!
craftRows.highlight: Trying something new was not bad at all!

craftRows.weak: And then we printed it out and then we checked for errors.
craftRows.strong: After we were satisfied, we printed a copy out and read it carefully to check if there were any errors.
